patch.value: Add pm brief since delta command for agent re-orientation after parallel/merge work

An agent resuming a project (especially after other agents worked in parallel branches and merged) needs a precise, token-budgeted summary of WHAT CHANGED since its checkpoint instead of re-reading the whole project. Adds a new 'pm brief since <checkpoint>' subcommand that classifies activity since a checkpoint into created / closed / status-transitions (incl. newly blocked, newly unblocked, started) / reprioritized / dependency-edge changes / notes+comments, grouped per item and joined with current item state. Directly serves the ecosystem philosophy 'project management = context management' and the multi-agent merge workflow.

pm brief since <checkpoint> summarizes changes since a checkpoint timestamp or relative window: created, closed, status transitions (newly blocked/unblocked/started), reprioritizations, dependency/edge changes, and new notes/comments; grouped per item with current title/type/status; supports --to, --author filter, --format text|json|markdown|slack, token budget, and deterministic ordering; sources from pm activity --full --json --from/--to joined with current item state; tests + README cover behavior; npm run release:check passes.

value.author: pi-agent
value.text: Shipped pm brief since <checkpoint> delta command: readActivitySince (pm activity --full --from/--to/--author/--limit), buildDelta classifier (created/closed/canceled/reopened/status-transition with started/newly-blocked/unblocked labels/reprioritized/retitled/reassigned/deps add-remove/notes+comments, deterministic changeRank+priority+recency+id ordering), renderMarkdownDelta/renderTextDelta/renderSlackDelta, token+item budget trimming reusing estimateTokens, brief since command registration with --until/--author/--limit/--max-items/--token-budget/--max-tokens/--format/--output. New exported types DeltaActivityEntry/DeltaItemChange/DeltaSummary. Added 6 buildDelta/render unit tests (all green). README updated with Delta briefs subsection. Gates: typecheck clean, 54/54 tests pass, pack dry-run ok, changelog:check ok; npm audit:prod blocked by environment EALLOWSCRIPTS policy (pre-existing, not code-related). Co-Authored-By: pi-agent <noreply@anthropic.com>

value.author: claude
value.text: Orchestrator review + verify pass (Claude). Reviewed pi's implementation and applied 4 fixes on top: (1) markdown '## Refresh' emitted a broken single-line ```cmd``` (unclosed code fence) → now a proper fenced block; (2) items appeared in MULTIPLE sections (created item with a note showed under both Created and Discussion) → partitioned into exactly one primary section via primaryDeltaSection/groupDeltaSections (token-efficient, deterministic); (3) bare relative checkpoints like '7d' SILENTLY matched nothing in pm activity (needs leading '-7d') → added normalizeCheckpoint() so 'pm brief since 7d' just works for agents; (4) creation baseline (create-event patch sets title/priority/status/assignee) was mis-counted as retitled/reprioritized/reassigned/status-changed (95 spurious 'retitled' on companion data) → create event now records only the created flag + title fallback, so only real post-create edits count. Added 3 regression tests (normalizeCheckpoint, create-baseline suppression, no-duplication) → 57/57 pass. Verified END-TO-END through the REAL CLI (installed as project extension in a throwaway workspace with seeded activity): markdown/text/json/slack formats, --author/--until filters, empty window, bad-format + missing-arg errors (rc=2, helpful messages). typecheck/build/test/changelog:check/pack:dry-run all green; audit:prod fails only on a LOCAL EALLOWSCRIPTS npm policy (zero dependency changes → identical to green main, passes in CI).
